rationale: In the hypothesis development section, Fang et al. (2016) is cited to support specific claims that central network positions improve information access and that brokerage can provide richer, more novel information linked to innovation outcomes. The citing paper uses these claims directly to motivate its H1/H2 predictions about information reach and information richness in TMSE mobility networks. This is more than a passing mention because the citation helps establish the conceptual logic behind the paper's core effects.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Fang et al. (2016) as prior evidence for how centrality and brokerage generate information advantages and performance-relevant innovation benefits. It then builds its own hypotheses by transferring that network-position logic to the TMSE mobility context and firm valuation outcomes.
